title: 10 best herbal teas for gerd
intro_desc: Herbal teas for gerd are infusions of plants and spices that can help alleviate the symptoms of gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD). These teas can reduce inflammation, soothe the digestive tract, and improve digestion, thereby reducing the frequency and severity of gerd symptoms. Some examples of herbal teas that help with gerd include chamomile, ginger, licorice root, and marshmallow root. For instance, chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, while ginger tea can help stimulate digestion and alleviate nausea. By incorporating these herbal teas into their daily routine, individuals with gerd can experience relief from their symptoms, leading to improved quality of life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with GERD because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus caused by acid reflux. It also has calming effects on the gastrointestinal tract, which can help relieve symptoms of GERD such as heartburn, bloating, and discomfort. Additionally, lemon balm tea has been shown to relax the sphincter muscle that separates the esophagus from the stomach, preventing acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Furthermore, lemon balm tea contains rosmarinic acid, which has been found to reduce gastric acid secretion, providing additional relief from GERD symptoms.

Licorice tea helps with GERD because it contains substances that can help balance the stomach's acid production. One of its key compounds, glycyrrhizic acid, can increase the mucus lining of the esophagus, providing a protective barrier against stomach acid. Additionally, licorice tea may help improve digestion and reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, further alleviating GERD symptoms. However, it is essential to consume deglycyrrhizinated licorice (DGL) tea or licorice products with the glycyrrhizic acid removed, as high levels of this compound can lead to side effects such as high blood pressure and potassium depletion.

Fennel tea helps with GERD because it has several properties that can soothe and protect the digestive tract. It contains volatile oils, such as anethole, which can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce spasms, thereby alleviating symptoms of acid reflux. Fennel tea also has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and protect the lining of the esophagus from damage caused by stomach acid. Additionally, fennel tea can stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, promoting healthy digestion and reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. Its carminative properties can also help relieve gas and bloating, providing further relief for individuals with GERD.

Horehound tea helps with GERD because it contains natural compounds that can provide relief from symptoms of gastroesophageal reflux disease. It has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can provide relief from heartburn and acid reflux. Horehound tea is also known to stimulate the production of digestive juices, which can help improve digestion and prevent the backflow of stomach acid into the esophagus. Additionally, horehound tea can help relax the muscles in the gastrointestinal tract, which can further prevent acid reflux and alleviate symptoms of GERD.

Thyme tea helps with GERD because it contains active compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These compounds can help soothe the inflammation in the esophagus caused by acid reflux and reduce the frequency and intensity of GERD symptoms. Additionally, thyme tea has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, which may help prevent the overgrowth of harmful bacteria in the stomach that can contribute to GERD. Furthermore, thyme tea can help stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, improving digestion and reducing the likelihood of acid reflux. Drinking thyme tea after meals may provide relief from GERD symptoms and promote overall digestive health.

Marjoram tea helps with GERD because it has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. It can also help to relax the muscles that control the opening and closing of the esophageal sphincter, preventing acid reflux. Additionally, marjoram tea has been shown to stimulate the production of digestive juices, improving digestion and reducing the symptoms of GERD. Its calming effect on the stomach and intestines can provide relief from discomfort and pain associated with the condition. Drinking marjoram tea regularly can help manage GERD symptoms and promote overall digestive health.

Rosemary tea helps with GERD because it contains carnosol and carnosic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining. Additionally, rosemary tea can help stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can improve digestion and reduce the production of stomach acid. This can help alleviate symptoms of GERD, such as heartburn, acid reflux, and stomach discomfort. Furthermore, rosemary tea has been shown to have a calming effect on the gastrointestinal system, which can help prevent the spasms and cramping that can worsen GERD symptoms.

Sage tea helps with GERD because it has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. Sage is also known to stimulate the production of digestive juices, which can improve digestion and help prevent acid reflux. Additionally, sage contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to have a protective effect on the lining of the stomach and esophagus, reducing the symptoms of GERD. Drinking sage tea regularly may help alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with GERD, providing relief and improving overall digestive health.

Large-leaved lime tea helps with GERD due to its natural anti-inflammatory and antacid properties. The tea is rich in citric acid, which can neutralize stomach acid and provide relief from acid reflux. Additionally, it contains flavonoids that help strengthen the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing acid from backing up into the esophagus. The herbal tea is also caffeine-free, making it a gentle and soothing option for those experiencing GERD symptoms. Drinking large-leaved lime tea can offer a natural and effective way to alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with gastroesophageal reflux disease.

Plantain tea helps with GERD because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the irritated esophageal lining caused by acid reflux. It contains mucilage, a type of fiber that creates a protective barrier in the stomach, preventing acid from splashing up into the esophagus. Plantain tea also has antacid effects, neutralizing stomach acid and promoting the healing of damaged tissue. Additionally, it helps to strengthen the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing acid from escaping the stomach. Drinking plantain tea can provide relief from GERD symptoms, such as heartburn, regurgitation, and difficulty swallowing.
